src.osir_lib.osir_lib.logger package

Submodules

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.logger.logger module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.logger.logger.CustomLogger(name, level=0)[source]

Bases: CustomLogger

__init__(name, level=0)[source]

Initialize the logger with a name and an optional level.

error_handler(ex: Exception, message='', *args, **kwargs)[source]

Captures detailed exception information, including the exact file and line number of the failure.

Parameters:

  • ex (Exception) – The exception object caught during execution.

  • message (str, optional) – An additional descriptive message explaining the context of the error.

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.logger.logger.LevelBasedHandler(level_formatter_map, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: StreamHandler

__init__(level_formatter_map, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Initialize the handler.

If stream is not specified, sys.stderr is used.

emit(record)[source]

Intercepts log records to apply specific formatters based on their severity level.

Parameters:

record (logging.LogRecord) – The log record containing the message and metadata.

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.logger.logger.LogFormatter(color, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Formatter

COLOR_CODES = {10: '\x1b[0;96m', 20: '\x1b[0;94m', 30: '\x1b[0;93m', 40: '\x1b[0;91m', 50: '\x1b[0;95m'}
ORIGIN_COLOR = '\x1b[1;36m'
RESET_CODE = '\x1b[0m'
__init__(color, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Initialize the formatter with specified format strings.

Initialize the formatter either with the specified format string, or a default as described above. Allow for specialized date formatting with the optional datefmt argument. If datefmt is omitted, you get an ISO8601-like (or RFC 3339-like) format.

Use a style parameter of ‘%’, ‘{’ or ‘$’ to specify that you want to use one of %-formatting, str.format() ({}) formatting or string.Template formatting in your format string.

Changed in version 3.2: Added the style parameter.

format(record, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Formats the log record by injecting OSIR-specific metadata like task IDs and component origins.

Parameters:

record (logging.LogRecord) – The record to be formatted.

Returns:

The fully formatted string ready for output to console or file.

Return type:

str

Module contents

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.logger.AppLogger(*args, **kwargs)

Retrieves or creates an instance of the class. This function checks if an instance already exists; if not, it creates a new one using provided arguments.

Parameters:

  • *args – Variable length argument list for class instantiation.

  • **kwargs – Arbitrary keyword arguments for class instantiation.

Returns:

The singleton instance of the class.

Return type:

object
